testCases.query: Calculate the ROI for a $500K software investment with $200K annual benefits over 5 years
expectedBehavior: Uses ROI formula: (Net Benefits - Total Costs) / Total Costs x 100%
Calculates net present value using an appropriate discount rate for the investment
Provides internal rate of return and payback period calculations
Includes Python code examples for automating the financial calculations

testCases.query: Compare build vs buy for an authentication system
expectedBehavior: Creates a 3-year total cost of ownership comparison table for each option
Includes hidden costs (opportunity cost, switching costs)
Provides sensitivity analysis with best-case, worst-case, and expected scenarios
Uses a structured business case template with executive summary and recommendation

testCases.query: Size the market for an AI developer tools product
expectedBehavior: Uses both top-down and bottom-up TAM calculation
Applies SAM filters (geography, segment, use case)
Calculates realistic SOM with competitive constraints
Cross-references top-down and bottom-up methods and reconciles any discrepancies

testCases.query: Analyze the competitive landscape for a project management SaaS
expectedBehavior: Applies Porter's Five Forces framework to assess industry competitive dynamics
Creates SWOT analysis with TOWS matrix
Builds a competitive positioning map plotting rivals on key differentiating axes
Includes a competitor profile template with strengths, weaknesses, and market share

testCases.query: Create a value proposition canvas for an enterprise collaboration tool
expectedBehavior: Maps customer jobs (functional, social, emotional)
Identifies pains and gains with ranking
Creates value map with pain relievers and gain creators
Assesses problem-solution fit by mapping pains and gains to product capabilities

testCases.query: Should we proceed with building an AI-powered search feature?
expectedBehavior: Uses a stage gate criteria checklist to evaluate readiness at each milestone
Applies a weighted scoring template covering feasibility, viability, and desirability
Provides decision thresholds (Go >= 7.0, No-Go < 5.0)
Includes a build, buy, or partner evaluation matrix with cost and risk tradeoffs

testCases.query: Prioritize these features: search improvements, dark mode, API v2, mobile app
expectedBehavior: Scores each feature with Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort
Calculates RICE score for each feature
Ranks features by calculated RICE score from highest to lowest priority
Documents the scoring rationale with justification for each factor assignment

testCases.query: Prioritize backlog items when we have a regulatory deadline
expectedBehavior: Calculates Cost of Delay (User Value + Time Criticality + Risk Reduction)
Divides by Job Size for WSJF score
Identifies time-sensitive items for higher priority
Includes MoSCoW categorization to distinguish must-have from nice-to-have items

testCases.query: Write OKRs for a team focused on improving customer retention
expectedBehavior: Creates qualitative and inspiring objectives that motivate the team toward outcomes
Defines quantitative key results with baselines and targets
Key results are outcome-focused, not output-focused
Targets are stretch goals (70% achievable)

testCases.query: Build a KPI tree for tracking SaaS revenue growth
expectedBehavior: Creates a hierarchical metric breakdown showing how sub-metrics roll up to top-level goals
Shows cause-effect relationships between leading input metrics and lagging outcomes
Identifies North Star metric with input metrics
Covers new revenue, expansion, and retention branches

testCases.query: Set up a balanced metrics dashboard for a product team
expectedBehavior: Distinguishes leading from lagging indicators and explains their predictive relationship
Shows the leading-to-lagging chain that connects team actions to business outcomes
Sets appropriate review cadences (weekly for leading, monthly for lagging)
Pairs actionable metrics with result metrics

testCases.query: Define and instrument a new KPI for feature adoption
expectedBehavior: Uses metric definition template with formula
Identifies data source, table, and owner
Defines metric segments and historical context for meaningful trend analysis
Sets alerting thresholds with warning and critical levels based on historical baselines

testCases.query: Create a user persona for a DevOps engineer using our CI/CD platform
expectedBehavior: Includes demographics, goals, pain points, behaviors
Adds a characteristic quote that captures the persona's mindset and priorities
Provides key insight for design decisions
Creates empathy map for specific scenario

testCases.query: Map the customer journey for onboarding new enterprise users
expectedBehavior: Defines five to seven journey stages from initial awareness through post-purchase
Maps actions, thoughts, emotions per stage
Identifies key pain points and improvement opportunities at each journey stage
Includes channel touchpoints and an emotional curve tracking user sentiment across stages

testCases.query: Plan a user research study to understand search behavior
expectedBehavior: Creates interview plan with research questions
Designs interview guide with warm-up, deep dive, wrap-up
Includes usability test plan with success criteria
Follows best practices (open-ended questions, no leading)

testCases.query: Write a PRD for a new notification system feature
expectedBehavior: Follows a standard PRD template structure with problem statement and scope
Includes user stories with INVEST criteria
Writes acceptance criteria in Given-When-Then format
Defines non-functional requirements and success metrics
